source.title: Striatal activity underlies novelty-based choice in humans

confidence_rationale: fMRI evidence in healthy humans that striatal activity underlies novelty-based choice even when novelty is unrelated to outcome value. Demonstrates that perceptual novelty drives choice via a striatal substrate, providing direct R2 anchor for one curiosity sub-flavour (novelty-driven). Key: the paper shows novelty-driven choice produces measurable individual differences -- a falsifiable signature.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that lacks novelty-driven choice bias should fail to reproduce striatal-equivalent activation during novel-option choice and should fail to show individual-difference variation in novelty susceptibility.

If novelty-driven choice is REDUNDANT with general curiosity (MECH-216 schema-readout-wanting), this paper would predict that lesioning the schema-readout pathway eliminates the novelty signature -- a testable cross-mechanism prediction.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Even when perceptual familiarity of an option is unrelated to choice outcome, novelty drives choice behaviour. Striatal activity underlies novelty-based choice in a manner consistent with computational accounts of decision making under uncertainty. The brain uses perceptual novelty to approximate choice uncertainty.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 MECH-314 candidate (curiosity bonus) needs at least one sub-mechanism for novelty-driven exploration with a striatal-equivalent substrate. REE's existing MECH-216 (E1 schema-readout-wanting) is the closest current candidate but is recognition-driven (schema MATCH triggers wanting), not novelty-driven (schema NOVEL triggers exploration). MECH-314a candidate: novelty-bonus-on-action-value via SD-014 VALENCE_WANTING write at unfamiliar z_world locations. Wittmann 2008 supports the architectural commitment that novelty deserves its own substrate channel within MECH-314.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper uses perceptual familiarity in a discrete-stimulus choice task. REE's continuous-state grid environment requires a continuous-novelty signal -- e.g., distance from frequently-visited z_world cells. The mapping is plausible but the operationalisation differs.
mapping.source_context: fMRI four-armed-bandit-with-novel-stimuli task in healthy adult humans; Wellcome Trust Centre for Neuroimaging UCL.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron venue, well-cited. Mapping fidelity moderate because perceptual-stimulus novelty differs from continuous-state-space novelty in REE's grid env.
